text: Matrix decompositions have been widely employed in data analysis for many years, notably based on multisensor information and ever increasing amount of data which have appeared. However, they seem no longer to be adequate; therefore one has to look for new tools. By representing the data by higher-order tensors (i.e., by multiway arrays) allows to introduce an entirely new class of more powerful polynomial models where -unlike the matrix case- uniqueness is ensured by very mild and common assumptions. Based on the remarkable capabilities provided by multilinear algebra, the data analysis methods based on tensor decompositions allow to fit easily to reasonable constraints which are often implicit in the data and to extract, from data, more general patterns than by using the matrix methods.
